$comment: Reusable secret-isolation fixture (KRT-BD003). Stages representative, non-real provider and MCP credentials confined to the integration edge. The KRT-BD004 secret-isolation check set configures these on a turn and the shared runner-owned secret-absence helper asserts none of `secretValues` (or their common encoded variants) appear in persisted kernel records, captured canonical stream events, captured telemetry attributes or error summaries, or the recorded transcript. `bridgeRequestToken` (KRT-BK004) is a JWT-shaped, pattern-detectable secret deliberately NOT added to `secretValues`: it must stay outside the value-equality list so the provider-bridge secret-isolation check's `secretPatternAbsence` assertion is the only thing that can catch it, genuinely proving structural (non-value-equality) detection rather than being redundantly also caught by `secretAbsence`.
